series_description: All medical records pertaining to Ohio Veterans Home residents including, but not limited to, patient assessments, medical orders, progress notes, laboratory results, physician orders, patient charts, powers of attorney, patient pharmacy records, medical history, records of physical examinations, treatment reports, admission, and discharge records.
retention_statement: Paper: 10 years from Resident death or discharge, unless scanned into electronic database ; Electronic: 10 years from Resident death or discharge

series_description: All admissions documents connected with the application for admission of a potential domiciliary resident. Documents include, but are not limited to, the DD214 attached to the application, birth certificate, Form 1010SH, transfer datasheet, admission application, application for health benefits, and clinical risk agreement.
retention_statement: Paper: 10 years from Resident death or discharge, unless scanned into electronic database ; Electronic: 10 years from Resident death or discharge

series_description: All documents needed in support of submitting claims for Veterans Benefits to the Department of Veterans Affairs pursuant to Title 38.
retention_statement: Paper: 10 years or until scanned into electronic form ; Electronic: retain indefinitely

series_description: All official police reports generated by the OVH Police Department during the course of their duties.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned or recorded electronically ; Electronic: retain indefinitely.

series_description: Video recordings made by the Ohio Veterans Home video recording system.
retention_statement: Electronic: 30 days unless saved for an authorized purpose including, but not limited to, an administrative, criminal, or civil investigation or other incident in which a video recording is useful. If such an incident occurs, retain until no longer useful, the statute of limitations has run, or any initiated action is final dependent upon the reason for the retention of the video. Unless such an incident occurs, the video may be overwritten after 30 days.

series_description: All records pertaining to the application and processing of Ohio Veterans Bonuses. These records include, but are not limited to, submitted applications, documents in support of the application, documents evidencing Department review of applications, and administrative documents tracking applications.
retention_statement: Electronic: Retain indefinitely. ; Paper: Retain until scanned or recorded electronically

series_title: Ohio Veterans Hall of Fame Nomination Packets/Records
series_description: All records pertaining to Ohio Veterans Hall of Fame nominations including, but not limited to, nomination packets, packet scoring documents in DVS possession, and voting records in DVS possession.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned or recorded electronically ; Electronic: Retain indefinitely.

series_description: Any Treasury/Fiscal document that is in paper form that has been scanned for electronic storage. This includes, but is not limited to, vouchers, receipts, warrant records, balance sheets, ledgers, and petty cash records. This series only applies to paper records that are newer than 2012 and that have been scanned for electronic storage. The Department has scanned all treasury/fiscal records electronically after 2012. It is the purpose of this Record Series to reduce duplication and physical storage costs where records are fully memorialized electronically. This Record Series does not apply to electronic records. Those records would be eligible for disposal per established Record Series specific to the record type.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain as per record specific record series unless it is scanned for electronic storage. Once scanned, the paper copy can be disposed of.

series_description: Paper copies of staff antigen testing
retention_statement: Paper: Retain until scanned ; Electronic: Retain 6 years after the date of the scan

series_description: Hardcopy requests for military records such as a DD-214.

copy of all eligibility records including but not limited to DD2014, standards of conduct forms, verification of training attendance, exam results, and accreditation documents from the United States Department of Veterans Affairs
retention_statement: Electronic: 5 years after Commissioner/Officer resignation, termination, death, or, for commissioners only, failing reappointment ; Paper: 5 years after Commissioner/Officer resignation, termination, death, or, for commissioners only, failing reappointment

series_description: Any Human Resources document in paper form that has been scanned into electronic storage or is otherwise duplicative of an electronic record. This includes, but is not limited to employee documents, employee applications, licensing records, position descriptions, grievance records, employment verification, benefits documents, on boarding checklists, and workers compensation. It is the purpose of this Record Series to reduce duplication and physical storage costs where records are fully memorialized electronically. This Record Series does not apply to electronic records. Those records would be eligible for disposal per established Record Series specific to the record type.
retention_statement: Paper: Retain as per record specific record series unless it is scanned for electronic storage. Once scanned, the paper copy can be disposed of.

series_description: Documents of a historical nature, including but not limited to blueprints, photographs, or other operational documents.
retention_statement: Paper: Indefinitely until reviewed by the State Archives. If the State Archives accepts, transfer to State Archives. ; Paper: If State Archives has reviewed and declined to take possession, the documents may be diposed of.

series_title: Military Injury Relief Fund Paper Files
series_description: All paper records pertaining to the application and processing of Military Injury Relief Fund applications. This includes medical files, DD-214s, and all other requested documentation.

All records pertaining to the duties of the State Approving Agency ("SAA") section of the Department. This includes but is not limited to, voyager reports, travel expenses, quarterly reports, and school catalogs. This schedule implements the contractual requirement of the cooperative agreement between DVS and the United States Department of Veterans Affairs. The retention period also allows sufficient time for audits and other reviews balanced against reducing long term storage costs for records with limited administrative value beyond the retention period.
